action.reasoning: Observation: Best result is safe_follow_steps=90.5 (iter 14) with PD control. Main bottleneck: robot can't maintain safe zone (1-2m, facing<0.5rad) long enough - needs 500 steps but only gets ~90. avg_dist=4.22m means robot spends too much time far away. Analysis: 1) Approach phase too slow due to aggressive angular gating, 2) PD gains in safe zone may be too aggressive causing oscillation, 3) Target distance 1.5m is center but robot tends to drift far. Decision: 1) Reduce angular gating during approach (allow more forward speed while turning), 2) Use less aggressive but more stable PD gains in safe zone, 3) Set target dist=1.4m to bias toward staying closer, 4) Increase angular gain in safe zone for better facing, 5) Earlier stuck recovery. Submit eval to measure improvement.
